name: Llama

dataUsage.details: Meta publicly states that its Llama/foundation models are trained using public content shared by adults on Meta products and that EU users can object to use of their public information and AI interactions for future model development, but I could not find a primary source specific to the Llama developer API stating whether customer/API inference data is used for training by default.

euAiAct.details: Meta has stated it has been working to ensure its AI offerings comply with Europe’s regulatory requirements and has delayed or limited some launches in Europe while engaging with EU regulators, but no primary-source statement was found claiming formal EU AI Act compliance for the Llama developer API.

notes: Primary-source information for the logged-in Llama developer portal was limited. Meta provides processor/data transfer terms and SCC-style transfer addenda in its legal terms, but I could not verify Llama API-specific data residency, retention, subprocessors, or API-data-training terms from a primary source, so those fields are left null or conservative defaults.
